title: Data Dictionary for nyc_311.csv
description:
**Description**

The dataset contains **10 000 New York City 311 complaint records** collected between **January 2010 and December 2020**.  
Each record is uniquely identified by an integer “Unique Key” that ranges from 15,636,252 to 48,471,253 but only 10 000 distinct values are present, indicating a sparse key space.  
Timestamps capture the full lifecycle of each complaint: when it was created, when it closed (≈2.7 % missing), and the scheduled due date (≈65 % missing).  
Agency information is provided both as abbreviations and full names, with NYPD, HPD, DOT, DSNY, DEP, and DOB handling the majority of cases.  
The dataset describes a variety of complaint types—Noise, Heat/Hot Water, Illegal Parking, Street Condition, etc.—and includes free‑text descriptors that often fall into an “Other” bucket, accounting for more than half of all entries.  
Location is partially geocoded; 25 % of records lack latitude/longitude pairs and over 17 % are missing street address components, yet the majority of coordinates lie within Manhattan’s typical latitudinal (40.11–40.91) and longitudinal (-77.5 to -73.7) ranges.

---

### Notable Characteristics

- **Central tendency & spread** – Created dates cluster around mid‑2015 (median ≈ 2015‑11‑12), while closed dates lag by a few months, reflecting typical resolution times.  
- **Distribution shape** – The date fields exhibit slight negative skewness (~‑0.03 to ‑0.08) and moderate coefficient of variation (~25–30 %), indicating long tails toward later years.  
- **Missing values** – Latitude/Longitude and address components are missing in ~25 % of records; Due Date is missing in ~68 %; Closed Date missing in ~2.7 %.  
- **Outliers & anomalies** – A handful of latitude/longitude pairs lie outside NYC (e.g., longitude ≈ ‑77.5), suggesting data entry errors or non‑NYC incidents that should be flagged.  
- **High “Other” categories** – Complaint Type and Descriptor have dominant “Other” buckets (>50 % each), implying many free‑text entries were not mapped to known categories.  
- **Duplicate keys** – The Unique Key column is guaranteed unique; no duplicate identifiers are present.  
- **PII/PHI considerations** – Records contain street addresses, intersection names, and geographic coordinates that may be considered personally identifying information under privacy regulations.  
- **Data quality issues** – Several categorical fields have very low cardinality yet high null counts (e.g., Vehicle Type almost all null), potentially reflecting incomplete source feeds or optional data collection.

---

*Attribution: Generated by qsv v21.1.0 describegpt

Unique Key.title: Unique Key
Unique Key.description: A unique integer identifier assigned to each 311 complaint record.

Created Date.title: Created Date
Created Date.description: The date on which the complaint was created in the system.

Closed Date.title: Closed Date
Closed Date.description: The date on which the complaint was closed in the system, if applicable.

Agency.title: Agency Abbreviation
Agency.description: Abbreviation code representing the agency responsible for handling the complaint.

Agency Name.description: Full name of the agency responsible for handling the complaint.
